title: [cross-impl] trickle half-interval bias for odd I
description: Python uses (interval+1)//2 for half in _begin_interval:line69; Rust/C use interval/2:line112 (rs), line38 (c). For I=5: Py transmits in [now+3,now+5), Rust/C in [now+2,now+4). RFC 6206 says [I/2,I); different distributions break test vector consistency across impls. See trickle.py:69, trickle.rs:112, trickle.c:38. Python comment claims RFC match but math differs from ports.
close_reason: Aligned all implementations on bias-free ceil(I/2) half-interval per RFC 6206 [I/2,I) for odd intervals; cross-impl tests and vectors now consistent. Commit 22e0a7eb0
